A friend of a friend is not necessarily an ally. Although Sudan currently has good relations with Iran, there is no evidence that it supports the Houthis. Sudan also has good relations with Saudi Arabia, the enemy of the Houthi armed forces. The Southern Transitional Council has always been an enemy of the Houthis.

Given the inconsistency of the 20,000 figure form the guardian, there doesn’t seem to be a pint in keeping it here when most sources including the one I’ve cited seem to confirm a marked increase from 2022 rather than an outrageous demobilization suggested by the guardians figures. It could be possible the number the guardian found was regarding a specific subset of Houthi fighters, possibly active duty, or specifically referring to their elite forces but it’s unclear.

Reasoning: "But last April, Pakistan's Parliament unanimously voted to decline a Saudi request to participate in its coalition fighting in Yemen against the allegedly Iranian-backed Houthi rebels. In response to edit request. I agree with their reasons, the source doesn't say China is the target. Additionally, the source is tabloid media, ambiguous, and contradictory. Furthermore, China has a naval base in the area and frequently has naval vessels in the area on routine patrols.
